Title: A Case of Gene Fragmentation in Plant Mitochondria Fixed by the Selection of a Compensatory Restorer of Fertility-Like PPR Gene

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Mitochondrial cytochrome c maturation factor CcmF N2 subunit; split fragment of ccmFN
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Mitochondria, heme-lyase complex / inner membrane-associated c-type cytochrome maturation machinery
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: c-type cytochromes (cytochrome c and cytochrome c1)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required for c-type cytochrome maturation supporting electron transport and respiration
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Translated mitochondrial transcript in wild type; translation strongly reduced in rfl8
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Interpretation aided by in vitro rescued rfl8 plantlets and comparison to wild type under standard growth conditions
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced ccmFN2 translation in rfl8 correlates with loss of the heme-lyase complex and absence of mature cytochrome c/c1
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ccmFN2 is a fragmented mitochondrial gene specific to Brassicaceae lineage context. 2. Its translation efficiency is specifically and strongly reduced in rfl8 mutants. 3. RFL8 binds the 5' region of ccmFN2 and is required for its expression. 4. Loss of ccmFN2 expression is linked to heme-lyase complex deficiency and failure of c-type cytochrome biogenesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Ribo-seq; RIP-seq; immunoblot detection of CcmFN2-dependent complex loss; complementation by RFL8 and Brassica rapa RFL8
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Defective expression is associated with embryo lethality and severe respiration defects
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Illustrates organelle gene fragmentation compensated by nuclear factors; relevant for cytonuclear compatibility and mitochondrial engineering
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Translation likely initiates at a non-AUG codon in a region corresponding to coding sequence in nonsplit ccmFN; not itself a nuclear gene, but a core experimental target in the gene–metabolism relationship

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome c maturation heme-lyase complex subunit
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: c-type cytochromes (cytochrome c and cytochrome c1)
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected as part of heme-lyase complex in wild type but not in rfl8
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of detectable heme-lyase complex signal in rfl8 indicates failure of CCM machinery accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CCMH marks the heme-lyase complex in BN-PAGE assays. 2. This complex is absent in rfl8 mutants. 3. Prior cited evidence indicates CCMH itself is essential for embryo development, supporting the physiological importance of the CCM pathway.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is a core component of the metabolic complex experimentally interrogated in Results, though not the primary mutated gene

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome c maturation heme-handling complex subunit
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: c-type cytochromes (cytochrome c and cytochrome c1)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports heme transport/handling for cytochrome c maturation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heme-handling complex detected at wild-type-like size and abundance in rfl8
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Not affected in rfl8, helping localize the defect specifically to the heme-lyase module rather than the heme-handling complex
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CCMA-containing heme-handling complex remains detectable in rfl8. 2. This contrasts with loss of the CCMH/CcmFN2-associated heme-lyase complex. 3. The result narrows the metabolic lesion to the heme-lyase branch of cytochrome c maturation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a directly assayed metabolic complex marker that helps define the site of nutrient/heme-protein maturation defect

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: PPR RNA-binding translational regulator (Rf-like PPR protein)
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Pentatricopeptide repeat (PPR) protein; Rf-like (RFL) subfamily; 13 P-type PPR repeats
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Promotes mitochondrial translation initiation/translation of ccmFN2 by direct RNA binding downstream of the proposed GTG start region
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential activator of ccmFN2 expression and thus indispensable for c-type cytochrome biogenesis and respiratory complexes III/IV accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates translation of ccmFN2; no direct support for nad2 intron 1 splicing despite indirect splicing reduction
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: c-type cytochrome maturation/biogenesis in mitochondria via control of heme-lyase complex assembly
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: c-type cytochromes (cytochrome c and cytochrome c1)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports mitochondrial electron transport, complex III structural integrity, electron transfer to complex IV, respiration, and embryo development
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro embryo rescue/tissue culture on sucrose-rich medium enabled survival of homozygous mutant plantlets; long-day greenhouse growth for standard plants
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts on formation of the mitochondrial heme-lyase complex indirectly through activation of ccmFN2 expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout/embryo-lethal mutant analysis; genetic complementation with RFL8-3HA and Brassica rapa RFL8; mitochondrial localization assay; BN-PAGE and immunoblotting; RT-qPCR; Ribo-seq; RIP-seq with and without RNase I; in vitro RNA gel mobility shift assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of RFL8 abolishes efficient ccmFN2 translation, eliminates heme-lyase complex accumulation, causes absence of cytochrome c/c1, strongly reduces complexes III and IV, and leads to embryo lethality
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RFL8 is an essential mitochondrial Rf-like PPR protein required for Arabidopsis embryo development. 2. rfl8 mutants fail to accumulate the heme-lyase complex and mitochondrial c-type cytochromes, with severe defects in respiratory complexes III and IV. 3. Ribo-seq showed a specific strong reduction in ccmFN2 translation efficiency in rfl8. 4. RIP-seq and gel-shift assays demonstrated direct association of RFL8 with the 5' region of ccmFN2 mRNA downstream of the proposed GTG start codon. 5. Brassica rapa RFL8 complements Arabidopsis rfl8, supporting conserved regulation of ccmFN2 in Brassicaceae.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: rfl8 loss causes embryo lethality, severe growth arrest, no roots, miniature bushy plantlets, and dependence on alternative respiration for survival in vitro
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Primarily fundamental rather than breeding-oriented; highlights that RFL-like PPR genes can evolve as essential compensatory factors for mitochondrial gene fragmentation and may be considered when engineering organelle gene expression or studying cytonuclear compatibility
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native Arabidopsis RFL8 promoter for RFL8-3HA complementation; Arabidopsis RFL8 promoter used to drive Brassica rapa RFL8 coding sequence in complementation
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Ribo-seq and RIP-seq; NCBI GEO: GSE166459; CATdb ProjectID: Blanc09_RIPseq_RFL8
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RFL8 is not a CMS restorer in this study but an essential compensatory RFL selected to enable expression of the fragmented mitochondrial gene ccmFN2 in Brassicaceae
